User Story: As a lifter, I want to see and switch between training days on the Log tab, so that I log only the exercises for the day I am training
Expected Behaviour: On load and after save, #dayTabs renders one role="tab" button per distinct day in state.program, sorted with numeric locale compare; the current day gets class active. Clicking a tab sets global day from data-day, re-runs renderTabs() and renderWorkout() only (workout cards and gauge/meta refresh for that day).
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want the app to recover if my selected day no longer exists, so that the Log tab never shows an empty or broken state
Expected Behaviour: When renderTabs() runs, if global day is not in days(), it is reset to days()[0] or "Day 1"; tabs and workout then render for that fallback day.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want one card per exercise on the active day, so that I can see prescription and log all sets in context
Expected Behaviour: renderWorkout() maps exercises() (filtered to current day, sorted by order then name) into article.exercise cards showing exercise name, sets×min-max reps · RIR 0-rirHigh meta, and primary-muscle tag; plus column headers Set/kg/reps/RIR and ex.sets set rows numbered 1..N.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter using assistive tech, I want labeled kg/reps/RIR inputs per set, so that I can enter data accurately on mobile
Expected Behaviour: Each set row renders three number inputs with data-k="{ex.id}_{n}_{load
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want sensible defaults when opening the Log tab, so that I can confirm or tweak rather than type everything from scratch
Expected Behaviour: For each set n, kg defaults to draft value if present, else fmt(recommendation.load ?? last-session set-n load ?? 0); reps default to draft or ex.min; RIR default to draft or 1. Recommendation load is omitted (null) for exercises with no history.
Test Result: Re-tested: new-lift kg shows blank placeholder; typing 80 reads "80"; reps select-on-focus replaces cleanly.
Issues / Notes: FIXED: blank kg default + select-on-focus on set inputs.

User Story: As a lifter, I want in-progress entries saved as I type, so that a refresh or accidental navigation does not lose my workout
Expected Behaviour: On any #workout input event, all workout input values are collected by data-k into an object and written to localStorage key repforge_draft_v1. On the next renderWorkout(), any draft key overrides the computed defaults for that field. Successful save removes the draft key entirely.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want to see my previous session sets for each exercise, so that I can compare against today attempt
Expected Behaviour: When last(ex.name) returns sets from the most recent log session for that exercise name (latest by date, then created), a Last: line shows each set as load×reps @rir; if no history, the block is omitted.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a new user, I want guidance when an exercise has no log history, so that I know how to pick a starting load
Expected Behaviour: With empty last(ex.name), recommendation() returns status:new, heat:0.12, chip label New lift, text instructing to pick a load for min-max reps at 0-rirHigh RIR, and load:null (no target kg appended). Card gets class is-new.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter who topped the rep range, I want a load increase suggestion, so that I progress when every set hit the top reps
Expected Behaviour: From last session: median load, minReps=min(reps), rir=avg(rirs). If minReps>=ex.max and rir<rirHigh+1: status:add, heat:0.82, label Add load, text to add weight, load=round(median+max(median*jumpPct/100, minJump)); recommendation paragraph appends Target {load} kg.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter who topped the range with reps in reserve, I want a larger jump suggestion, so that I capitalize on an easy session
Expected Behaviour: If minReps>=ex.max and avg(rirs)>=rirHigh+1: status:add2, heat:1, label Add load ++, bold-jump copy, load=round(median+max(median*jumpPct*2/100, minJump)). Takes precedence over plain add. Card class is-add2.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter still inside the rep range, I want to keep load and chase reps, so that I follow double progression
Expected Behaviour: If history exists and minReps>=ex.min but minReps<ex.max: status:hold, heat:0.48, label Hold · add reps, hold-and-add-reps text, load = last session median (unchanged). Prefilled kg uses that load.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter who missed the bottom of the range, I want a back-off signal, so that I rebuild instead of forcing load
Expected Behaviour: If minReps<ex.min: status:reduce, heat:0.18, label Back off, text that a set dropped below min reps and to hold or trim load; load remains last session median (not auto-reduced). Card class is-reduce.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want a visual heat meter and status chip on each exercise, so that I can scan progression readiness at a glance
Expected Behaviour: Each card renders .heat__fill at width round(recommendation.heat*100)% and a .chip with recommendation.label; article class is-{status} drives CSS heat styling (is-new, is-hold, is-add, is-add2, is-reduce).
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want a session-level heat indicator in the header, so that I see how many lifts on this day are ready to add load
Expected Behaviour: updateGauge() counts current-day exercises whose recommendation status is add or add2; sets #heatGauge .gauge__fill width to (hot/total)*100% (0% if no exercises), label to {hot} hot when hot>0 else forge, and toggles class is-hot on the gauge when hot>0.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want the save button to show day and set count, so that I confirm scope before committing
Expected Behaviour: #saveMeta text is {current day} · {N} sets where N is the sum of sets for all exercises on the active day; updated whenever the workout renders.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want to set the workout date, so that I can backdate or correct the session date
Expected Behaviour: #date is a type="date" input; init() sets it to today() (local YYYY-MM-DD). On save, #date.value is used, falling back to today() if empty.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want optional session notes, so that I can record context with the workout
Expected Behaviour: #notes textarea accepts free text; on save its trimmed value is copied onto every logged set row notes field, then the textarea is cleared. Draft autosave does not persist notes.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want to commit the Log form and persist all sets, so that my session appears in Stats and History
Expected Behaviour: Submitting #logForm calls saveWorkout(): prevents default; builds session id {date}_{day}_{timestamp}; for each exercise and each set pushes a row with name, set number, parsed load/reps/rir (NaN to 0), shared notes, created ISO timestamp; appends to state.log and saves; clears draft and notes; toggles .btn--save.is-stamped animation; toast Workout forged — N sets logged.; calls full render().
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want four overview metrics on the Stats tab, so that I can see my overall training volume at a glance
Expected Behaviour: renderStats() fills #metrics with four tiles: Sessions = count of unique session IDs; Sets logged = state.log.length; Volume = kfmt(sum of load×reps) with kg suffix; Best e1RM = fmt(round(max e1rm)) with kg and metric--hot styling.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want a dropdown of every logged exercise, so that I can pick which lift to analyze
Expected Behaviour: #statExercise is rebuilt from unique exercise names in state.log, sorted alphabetically; if the log is empty a single No data option is shown; changing the select triggers renderStats() via onchange.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want my exercise choice to survive re-renders, so that saving a workout does not reset the chart
Expected Behaviour: Before rebuilding options, renderStats() stores the current #statExercise value in old; after repopulating, it restores old only when names.includes(old); otherwise first option applies.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want each exercise aggregated per workout session, so that charts and tables reflect session-level performance
Expected Behaviour: summaries() groups state.log rows by session
Source: name; computes top=max load (topReps at that load), volume=sum(load×reps), e1rm=best e1rm, reps=sum, rir=avg, sets=count; sorted by date then session ascending.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want a text summary of load progression, so that I can see direction without reading the chart
Expected Behaviour: When rows exist, #trend shows Top load first→latest kg, a delta span with class up/down and ▲/▼/· plus abs(delta) kg over N session(s), and Best e1RM kg; when no rows, #trend is cleared.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want guidance when no data exists for the selected exercise, so that I know what to do next
Expected Behaviour: draw() clears the canvas and, when rows.length===0, draws centered steel text Log this lift to chart its progression. then returns without axes/series.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want a readable Y-axis scale, so that load values are interpretable
Expected Behaviour: With data, draw() maps index to X (single point centered; multiple spread across) and top to Y using lo=max(0,min-25%span) and hi=max+25%span; draws 4 gridlines with right-aligned Y labels; X-axis shows shortDate first and last.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want a visually rich progression line, so that recent progress stands out
Expected Behaviour: draw() fills ember gradient area under line; strokes quench→gold→white gradient line; gold r3 points, white r5 last point with ember shadow; labels latest top as {top}kg offset by edge proximity.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want a table of my last sessions for the selected exercise, so that I can review recent numbers quickly
Expected Behaviour: #recent renders via table() the last 8 summary rows (newest first) with columns Date, Top, Reps, RIR, e1RM, Vol; empty rows show No data yet.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want a leaderboard of my heaviest set per exercise, so that I can compare best loads across lifts
Expected Behaviour: #tops builds one row per logged exercise name: the raw log entry with highest load for that name, sorted by load descending; columns Exercise, kg, Reps, RIR, Date; empty log yields No data yet.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want consistent numeric display in Stats, so that values are readable and comparable
Expected Behaviour: e1rm(load,reps)=load×(1+reps/30) when both>0 else 0; fmt() renders ints plainly and decimals trimmed; kfmt() shows >=10000 as k otherwise round; used across tiles, trend, chart, tables.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want one card per saved workout sorted by most recent first, so that I can quickly find my latest sessions
Expected Behaviour: renderHistory() deduplicates state.log into one object per unique session id via Map, then sorts descending by created. Each session renders as a .session card inside #sessions; latest created appears first.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

Feature: Session card day date set count
User Story: As a lifter, I want each session card to show training day, date, and number of sets, so that I can scan sessions at a glance
Expected Behaviour: Each card shows s.day in .session__day and s.date plus sets.length labeled sets in .session__sub as {date} · {n} sets · …; all dynamic text passed through esc().
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want to see the heaviest set in each session, so that I can compare peak performance
Expected Behaviour: For each session, sets reduce to the row with strictly greatest numeric load; card shows fmt(top.load)×top.reps in .session__stat. If no set has load>0, shows 0×0.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want total session volume on each card, so that I can gauge overall training load
Expected Behaviour: Session volume = sum((load
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a new user, I want a clear message when no workouts exist, so that I know how to start logging
Expected Behaviour: When the deduplicated sessions array is empty, #sessions shows .table>.empty with text No sessions yet. Forge your first on the Log tab.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want a confirmation before deleting a session, so that I do not remove data by accident
Expected Behaviour: Each card has a Delete button (.session__del) with data-del=session id. Click runs confirm(Delete this session? This cannot be undone.); cancel leaves state.log unchanged with no save/render/toast.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want a confirmed delete to remove all sets for that session and update the app, so that history stays accurate
Expected Behaviour: On confirm OK: state.log filters out rows with that session; save() persists; render() re-runs; toast Session deleted. Both #sessions and #historyTable reflect removal immediately.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

Feature: Every set table columns and order
User Story: As a lifter, I want a full ledger of every logged set in a sortable table, so that I can audit individual sets
Expected Behaviour: #historyTable filled by table(rows) where rows is all state.log sorted by date desc, name asc, set asc. Columns Date, Day, Exercise, Set, kg, Reps, RIR; escaped.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a user with no logged sets, I want the Every set section to show an empty message, so that the table area is not blank
Expected Behaviour: When state.log is empty, table() returns div.empty No data yet. inside #historyTable.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want a dedicated Program view with editor, advanced JSON, and volume audit, so that I can manage my split in one place
Expected Behaviour: Program tab shows #program: lede; #programEditor; +Add day; collapsible Advanced details with #programJson and Save JSON; Weekly volume audit heading with #volume. renderProgram() calls renderProgramEditor() then renderVolume().
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want my program rendered as one card per training day with nested exercise cards, so that I can scan and edit my split by day
Expected Behaviour: renderProgramEditor() reads prog.days() sorted numeric; each day via dayCard(d); each exercise from prog.forDay(d) via exCard(e,i,n). Day cards .pday; exercises .pex inside .pexlist.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter starting fresh, I want a clear empty state when I have no training days, so that I know to add a day first
Expected Behaviour: When prog.days() is empty, #programEditor shows No training days yet. Add one to start building your split. (in .table/.empty).
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want to add a new training day with one starter exercise, so that I can expand my split quickly
Expected Behaviour: +Add day calls prog.addDay(): next unused Day N name, pushes Exercise (order 1, New exercise, sets 3, min 6, max 10). Sets global day, persistProgram(), full render(), toast Day added.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want to rename a day inline, so that my split labels match how I train
Expected Behaviour: Inline input (data-act=renameDay). On change, prog.renameDay(old,new) trims; succeeds only if non-empty and different; updates all matching exercises and renumber(). On success, if active day matched old it switches; persistProgram(), render(), toast Day renamed. On failure resets input.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want to delete a day and all its exercises after confirmation, so that I can remove unused days safely
Expected Behaviour: Day ✕ runs confirm(Delete {day} and all of its exercises?). If confirmed, prog.removeDay(day), persistProgram(), render(), toast Day deleted. Cancel unchanged.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want each day card to show exercise and set totals, so that I can gauge day volume at a glance
Expected Behaviour: dayCard computes exs and sets=sum(e.sets); header shows {exs.length} ex · {sets} sets. Empty day shows No exercises yet. Add your first below.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want to add a new exercise to a specific day, so that I can grow that day template
Expected Behaviour: +Add exercise calls prog.addExercise(day): order=max order+1 (or 1); defaults New exercise, sets 3, min 6, max 10. persistProgram(), render(), toast Exercise added.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want name and muscle fields to update as I type, so that edits feel immediate
Expected Behaviour: Inputs with data-field name/primary/secondary bind oninput: prog.update assigns string as-is; persistProgram(), renderVolume(), updateGauge(), updateSaveMeta() — editor not fully re-rendered, preserving focus.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want sets/min/max to stay valid positive integers, so that my program never stores garbage
Expected Behaviour: Number inputs (min=1, step=1) use oninput path; Program.update applies posInt(value,current). On change/blur each number input resets from model so invalid values revert. Live update does not enforce max>=min.
Test Result: Re-tested: Min 12 -> Max becomes 12; Max 5 -> Min becomes 5; abc/-3/blank Sets revert to stored value.
Issues / Notes: FIXED: Program.update enforces max>=min; onchange re-syncs sibling number fields; select-on-focus.

User Story: As a lifter, I want to move exercises up or down within their day, so that I can control session order
Expected Behaviour: Each exCard shows ▲(up) and ▼(down); first ▲ disabled, last ▼ disabled. Click prog.move(id,±1) swaps order with neighbor; no-op at bounds. persistProgram(), render().
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want to remove an exercise from a day, so that I can drop movements I no longer train
Expected Behaviour: Exercise ✕ (delEx) calls prog.removeExercise(id) (filter + renumber), persistProgram(), render(), toast Exercise removed.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a power user, I want the advanced textarea to reflect the program without clobbering my typing, so that I can inspect or bulk-edit safely
Expected Behaviour: On each renderProgramEditor(), if activeElement is not #programJson, textarea set to JSON.stringify(prog.toJSON().map(drop id),null,2). While focused, sync skipped. Advanced block in details.advanced with warning lede.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a power user, I want to replace the entire program by parsing the JSON textarea, so that I can import or bulk-edit a split
Expected Behaviour: Save JSON runs saveProgram(): JSON.parse must yield an array or throw. On success prog=new Program(parsed) (normalizes via Exercise), persistProgram(), sets day to first day, render(), toast Program saved. On failure toast That JSON didn't parse. Check the brackets and commas.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want a per-muscle weekly volume summary with bars, so that I can spot over/under-trained groups
Expected Behaviour: renderVolume() builds prog.volume(): primary muscles +sets direct; secondary +sets*0.5; effective=d+p. Sorted desc. Each row: name, bar width max(4,round(eff/max*100))%, label {eff} sets. eff>=10 adds is-high. Empty shows No exercises in the program.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want every program change saved to localStorage immediately, so that my split survives refresh
Expected Behaviour: persistProgram() sets state.program=prog.toJSON() and save(). Invoked after field edits, editor actions, add day, JSON save. Text-field edits also refresh volume and save metadata.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter upgrading from a legacy backup, I want program metadata synthesized on load, so that my split gains identity without losing data
Expected Behaviour: When state lacks programMeta, normalizeLoaded creates { id, name:"", started: earliest log date or null, created, updated }. Persisted on next save or boot.
Test Result: Simulation: delete programMeta → reload → id restored.

User Story: As a lifter, I want name, start date, and status on the Program tab, so that I see what program I am running
Expected Behaviour: #programMeta renders editable name and started date plus read-only chips: week (if started), status label, adherence (N/M days this week), progression health, volume compliance.
Test Result: Simulation: name edit persists; adherence chip visible.

User Story: As a coach, I want program export to include metadata, so that shared templates carry a name and start anchor
Expected Behaviour: exportProgram() writes { version:2, meta, exercises }. importProgramFile accepts v2, legacy array, or { program } shapes.
Test Result: Simulation: v2 round-trip with meta; legacy array import.

User Story: As a lifter, I want the Settings tab to show my saved progression dials, so that I can review them before editing
Expected Behaviour: On every renderSettings(), #jumpPct, #minJump, #rirHigh are set from state.settings values.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a user, I want to see where my data lives, so that I understand there is no cloud copy
Expected Behaviour: renderSettings() sets #storageNote explaining data is stored under localStorage key repforge_v1 and advising export before clearing site data or switching phones.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a lifter, I want to save Load jump %, Minimum jump (kg), and Target RIR ceiling, so that recommendations and rounding use my preferences
Expected Behaviour: #saveSettings reads three inputs, coerces with +, applies fallbacks (jumpPct/minJump default 2.5 if falsy; rirHigh default 2), assigns state.settings, save(), render(), toast Settings saved.
Test Result: Re-tested: RIR ceiling 0 and jump%% 0 persist after reload; min-jump 0 correctly floored to 2.5.
Issues / Notes: FIXED: settings validated with Number.isFinite + range; min-jump kept > 0.

User Story: As a user, I want to download a full JSON backup, so that I can restore program, settings, and log elsewhere
Expected Behaviour: #exportJson runs exportJson(): JSON.stringify(state,null,2) to download() as application/json named repforge_backup_<date>.json.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a user, I want my training log as CSV, so that I can analyze sets in a spreadsheet
Expected Behaviour: #exportCsv builds CSV header session,date,day,name,set,load,reps,rir,notes,created; each row quoted with internal quotes doubled; downloads as text/csv named repforge_log_<today>.csv (header-only if empty).
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a user, I want to restore from a backup file, so that I can recover or migrate my data
Expected Behaviour: #importJson reads first file, parses JSON, succeeds only if s.program exists and s.log is array; replaces state, rehydrates prog, normalizes state.program, sets day to first day, save(), render(), toast Backup imported., clears file input.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a user, I want clear feedback when an import fails, so that I know my current data was not replaced
Expected Behaviour: If no file, parse fails, s.program missing, or s.log not array, importJson() toasts That file isn't a valid RepForge backup., leaves state unchanged, resets input.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a user, I want to wipe all logged sets with confirmation, so that I can start fresh without accidental loss
Expected Behaviour: #reset opens confirm(Delete the training log? Export a backup first if you need it.); on OK sets state.log=[], save(), render(), toast Log deleted.; program and settings not cleared.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a user, I want to switch between Log, Stats, History, Program, and Settings, so that I can reach each section
Expected Behaviour: Each nav button has data-view matching a .view section id; click toggles active on exactly one nav button and one view; defaults Log active.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a user, I want the app refreshed when I change tabs, so that I always see current data at the top
Expected Behaviour: After active-state toggling, window.scrollTo({top:0}) runs and render() re-renders all views.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a user, I want a persistent top bar with branding, so that I always know which app I am using
Expected Behaviour: header.topbar shows brand link (aria-label RepForge home) with mark and REP/FORGE wordmark; topbar__end holds #heatGauge and initially hidden #installBtn.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a user, I want to install RepForge to my home screen, so that it launches like a native app
Expected Behaviour: beforeinstallprompt preventDefault(), stores event, unhides #installBtn; click runs prompt(), awaits userChoice, nulls installPrompt, re-hides button.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a user, I want brief inline feedback messages, so that actions confirm success or failure without blocking
Expected Behaviour: toast(m) sets #toast text, removes hidden, clears prior timeout, re-adds hidden after 2400ms; used across many flows.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a user, I want the service worker registered on load, so that offline caching can activate
Expected Behaviour: On init, if serviceWorker in navigator, register(./sw.js) runs; errors silently ignored.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a user, I want core app files cached, so that RepForge works offline after the first visit
Expected Behaviour: sw.js install opens cache repforge-v3 and addAll(ASSETS); activate deletes other keys and clients.claim(); GET fetch serves cache-first, else network with runtime put, else falls back to cached ./index.html.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a user, I want my saved data restored on launch, so that sessions persist across reloads
Expected Behaviour: load() parses localStorage KEY; returns object only if s.program exists and s.log is array; else returns default settings + built-in program + empty log.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a user, I want mutations persisted immediately, so that data survives browser restarts
Expected Behaviour: save() writes JSON.stringify(state) to localStorage KEY repforge_v1; invoked after every state mutation.
Test Result: Re-confirmed in browser post-fix; no regression.

User Story: As a user, I want correct PWA install metadata, so that the installed app has the right name, colors, and scope
Expected Behaviour: index.html links manifest and sets theme-color plus Apple meta; manifest defines name/short_name RepForge, standalone portrait, start_url ./index.html, scope ./, colors #0e1116, icon.svg any maskable.
Test Result: Re-tested: no apple-mobile deprecation warning in console after cache refresh.
Issues / Notes: FIXED: added mobile-web-app-capable meta; SW cache bumped to repforge-v4.
